claim_status: Benchmark readiness-review template only; no owner review, no performance review, no benchmark operations review, no quality review, no security review, no accessibility review, no release-operations review, no approved hardware tiers, no clean OS-control review, no representative corpus review, no browser-run server evidence review, no implemented browser launch runner review, no benchmark-ready browser pin review, no trace-artifact review, no raw result review, no 30-tab artifact review, no statistics review, no denominator review, no equal-workload review, no claim-bundle review, no benchmark-ready status, no public performance claim, no faster claim, no lower-memory claim, no lower-energy claim, no Chrome-class claim, no competitor-result claim, no daily-driver claim, no production claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Placeholder reviewers, self-approval, null review fields, and this no-claim PB-013 template cannot be used as benchmark readiness, benchmark-ready browser pins, public claim, performance result, or implementation evidence.

hardware_os_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed Tier L fixed hardware manifest beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template, including CPU, GPU, RAM, display, storage, firmware, owner, replacement policy, and artifact storage boundary.

Owner-reviewed Tier M fixed hardware manifest beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template, including product-gate role, power profile, thermal capture, display normalization, and reproducible identity.
Owner-reviewed Tier H approval or replacement policy beyond the checked current-host candidate and checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Clean OS image or restore procedure for each approved hardware tier, with update freeze, target version, quality update policy, driver freeze, firmware freeze, and rollback evidence beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Approved power, display refresh, scale, brightness, thermal, clock, sleep, background service, and network isolation controls beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Reviewed driver and firmware inventory with hashes, update policy, replacement triggers, and unsupported-degraded-mode statements beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Approved artifact storage root, retention policy, redaction boundary, SHA-256 manifest behavior, and no-real-profile rule beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Owner, benchmark operations, performance, quality, security, accessibility, and release-operations review of hardware and OS controls beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

corpus_network_axes.required_evidence: Reviewed representative offline corpus beyond the generated no-claim seed and checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template, covering page, app, accessibility, media, hostile, service-worker, and international cases.

Approved corpus denominator with disabled cases, unsupported cases, expected failures, fixture hashes, licenses, input scripts, viewports, and rerun triggers beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Browser-run server evidence beyond the checked runner-managed server lifecycle self-test and checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template, including startup, route checks, Host/DNS execution, cleanup, and failure records.

HTTP/2, HTTP/3, TLS, certificate, proxy, authentication, cache-revalidation, latency, loss, bandwidth, and network shaping profiles beyond the checked HTTP/1.1 no-claim profile and benchmark-readiness-review template.
Reviewed DNS override, loopback-only, external network blocking, cache, service-worker, and network isolation evidence beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Accessibility, keyboard, screen-reader, high-contrast, reduced-motion, and zoom workload fixtures beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template when performance claims touch everyday or accessible use.
Crash, revival, recovery, trace, DevTools, source-map, debugger, and diagnostics workload coverage beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template when claims touch those workflows.
Agent observation, action, provider, token, memory, GPU, and cost workload coverage beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template when claims touch AI or agent workflows.

Owner, benchmark operations, performance, quality, security, accessibility, and release-operations review of corpus and network controls beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Explicit unsupported and degraded network modes for TLS, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, proxy, authentication, cache, DNS, service worker, live-site, and external network cases beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

runner_artifact_axes.required_evidence: Implemented browser launch runner beyond the checked no-browser self-test and checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template, with timeout, cancellation, repetitions, warmup, sample order, cache reset, viewport, target resolution, and no-claim finalization.

Runner-owned temporary-profile creation, prohibited-path checks, no-real-profile proof, fake profile fixtures, cleanup checks, and residual-artifact records beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Runner-managed server, browser process, trace collector, timeout, crash, cancellation, cleanup, and failure-finalization records beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Runner-generated raw samples, raw result files, sample order, warmup samples, failure records, unsupported records, and non-sample artifact package beyond the checked sample manifest and benchmark-readiness-review template.

Runner-generated trace package with ETW or equivalent traces, Perfetto-compatible traces where applicable, logs, screenshots, memory snapshots, power data, raw samples, artifact hashes, redaction review, and retention decision beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Runner-generated 30-tab mixed-state and all-live artifacts with tab IDs, navigation order, lifecycle timestamps, process topology, revival measurements, state-loss evidence, and cleanup records beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Browser trace instrumentation, platform counter collection, per-process and per-tab attribution, shared-resource charging, GPU accounting, energy samples, unknown-bucket thresholds, and UI/reporting fixtures beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Negative tests proving forbidden arguments, real profile paths, hidden failures, missing claim metadata, unequal workload settings, disabled security mitigations, missing artifact hashes, and cleanup failures fail closed beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Owner, performance, benchmark operations, quality, security, accessibility, and release-operations review of runner and artifact output beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Explicit implementation boundary showing which runner paths are real, which are no-browser self-tests, which are sample-only, and which cannot support benchmark-ready or performance claims beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

browser_pin_comparison_axes.required_evidence: Benchmark-ready Turing browser pin with build ID, channel, commit, command line, profile, settings, sandbox, site isolation, cache, lifecycle, and artifact hash evidence beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Owner-reviewed benchmark-ready Chrome and Edge browser pins with browser-reported versions, channel proof, update state, command lines, profiles, settings, sandbox, site isolation, memory-saver, energy-saver, and cleanup evidence beyond the checked diagnostic records and benchmark-readiness-review template.

Owner-reviewed Firefox, Safari, and Safari Technology Preview local evidence where in claim scope, including executable identity, browser-reported versions, channel proof, settings, command lines, profiles, and cleanup evidence beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Release-catalog, local install, browser-reported version, channel, update ring, profile, settings, and executable-hash reconciliation beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Equal-workload review covering corpus hashes, suite versions, routes, input scripts, viewport, DPR, display refresh, sample order, cache, lifecycle state, and unsupported denominator beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Equal-security review covering sandbox, site isolation, certificate validation, mitigations, JIT mode, extension state, profile state, account/sync state, memory saver, energy saver, background throttling, and disabled-feature exceptions beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Owner-reviewed actual equal-workload competitor runs with retained raw artifacts, failure denominator, and claim boundaries beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Owner, performance, benchmark operations, quality, security, accessibility, and release-operations review of browser pins and comparisons beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

statistics_denominator_axes.required_evidence: Recorded sample count, repetitions, warmup policy, measured sample order, randomization, cold/warm state, and cache state beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Confidence intervals, uncertainty statement, variance, effect size, practical significance, outlier policy, noise study, and raw-sample retention beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Failure denominator records for failed, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, revived, state-loss, wrong-target, trace-finalization, cleanup, and profile-isolation cases beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Separate mixed-state and all-live 30-tab denominator records covering live, throttled, frozen, serialized, discarded, crashed, protected, audio, unsaved-work, and state-loss cases beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Physical, resident, committed, reserved, shared, compressed, swapped, semantic charged memory, GPU memory, and unknown-bucket accounting beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.
Power, battery, wakeup, thermal, GPU, display, local AI, and agent-cost energy accounting beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template where an energy claim is in scope.

Accessibility tree, screen-reader, keyboard, high contrast, reduced motion, zoom, and localization overhead records beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template where everyday or accessibility claims are in scope.

DevTools, tracing, diagnostics, protocol, source-map, debugger, extension, Plug-in, automation, agent, model, token, local-model memory, GPU, and provider-cost overhead records beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template where those claims are in scope.

Owner, performance, benchmark operations, quality, security, accessibility, and release-operations review of statistics and denominator controls beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Published denominator size, unsupported behavior, failed cases, excluded cases, residual risk, raw artifact hashes, and rerun triggers beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template before any public claim.

claim_review_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed claim bundle generated from real results beyond the checked no-claim claim-bundle template and checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Owner-approved exact public copy, supported scope, supported hardware, workload versions, browser versions, sample counts, unsupported behavior, residual risk, and publication diff beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Expiry date, rerun triggers, removal policy, competitor update triggers, suite version triggers, hardware/OS/control triggers, and stale-claim cleanup proof beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Performance reviewer accepts methods, raw artifacts, statistics, uncertainty, effect size, denominator, resource attribution, and claim scope beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Benchmark operations reviewer accepts hardware, OS, corpus, network, runner, browser pins, artifact storage, cleanup, retention, and rerun controls beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Quality and security reviewers accept failure denominator, equivalent security settings, sandbox/site-isolation disclosure, negative runner tests, redaction, and prohibited-public-claim boundaries beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Accessibility and release-operations reviewers accept accessibility overhead scope, release-copy boundaries, support language, expiry cleanup, and publication controls beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

Owner accepts whether PB-013 remains documented_no_runner or can be promoted, and records that validation alone cannot support benchmark-ready, Chrome-class, public performance, production, daily-driver, or implementation claims beyond the checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim benchmark-readiness-review template is cited as owner-reviewed benchmark readiness, PB-013 readiness, benchmark-ready status, public performance, Chrome-class, faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, competitor-result, daily-driver, production, or implementation evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject readiness promotion and all benchmark, performance, product, production, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Owner, performance, benchmark operations, quality, security, accessibility, or release-operations reviewer fields are placeholders, self-approved, null in a real review, or predate the latest artifacts.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark readiness and public claim eligibility.

rejection_rules.condition: Tier L, Tier M, Tier H approval or replacement policy, clean OS image, update freeze, driver freeze, firmware freeze, display, thermal, network isolation, or artifact storage evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark-ready status and performance claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Representative offline corpus, page/app/accessibility/media/hostile/service-worker/international coverage, fixture hashes, disabled-case denominator, or owner review is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark-ready status and Chrome-class or public performance claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Browser-run server evidence, DNS execution proof, benchmark-run server artifacts, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, TLS, proxy, authentication, cache-revalidation, or network shaping evidence is missing where required by the claim.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark-ready status and network-sensitive claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Implemented browser launch runner, timeout, cancellation, cache reset, temporary-profile isolation, prohibited-path check, no-real-profile proof, trace capture, cleanup, or result finalization is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark results and public claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Runner-generated raw samples, raw result files, sample count, warmup policy, statistics, trace package, artifact hashes, or retention decision is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark result, performance, memory, energy, and Chrome-class claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Benchmark-ready browser pins, browser-reported versions, channel proof, settings, command lines, profile state, update state, cleanup, or competitor reconciliation is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject competitor result and public comparison claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Workload versions, corpus hashes, routes, viewports, cache state, lifecycle state, process topology, browser settings, or sample order differ outside an approved scope.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject comparison and public performance claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Sandbox, site isolation, certificate validation, JIT, mitigations, memory saver, energy saver, extension state, account state, profile state, or disabled-feature settings differ without explicit experimental labeling.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject public performance and Chrome-class claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Hidden failures, unsupported cases, crashes, timeouts, cancellations, discards, revivals, state loss, wrong-target launches, trace failures, cleanup failures, or unknown resource buckets are omitted from the denominator.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject all benchmark and product claims.

rejection_rules.condition: The runner reads or writes real user profiles, cookies, history, sync data, extensions, account state, secrets, credentials, or production profile paths.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the run and retain only sanitized diagnostic evidence if safe.

rejection_rules.condition: Owner-reviewed claim bundle, exact public copy, expiry policy, rerun triggers, unsupported behavior, residual risk, or public-copy diff is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject public performance, Chrome-class, faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, competitor-result, daily-driver, production, and release claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Claim bundle, browser versions, benchmark suite versions, corpus hashes, hardware, OS, driver, firmware, power, thermal, display, network, sandbox, lifecycle, or browser-version inputs have expired or changed.
rejection_rules.outcome: Remove or reject public claim language until rerun evidence exists.

rejection_rules.condition: Repository validation, manifest validation, self-tests, no-browser runner output, no-claim templates, smoke runner output, or diagnostic browser-pin captures are cited as benchmark results.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark-ready status and public performance claims.

unsupported_boundaries: no approved Tier L, Tier M, or Tier H hardware set exists
no clean OS image or approved update-control manifest exists
no reviewed representative offline corpus exists
no implemented browser benchmark launch runner exists
no equal-workload or equal-security comparison exists
no owner-reviewed claim bundle generated from real results exists
no public performance claim is supported
no faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, Chrome-class, competitor-result, daily-driver, production, or implementation claim is supported
